Ad Text:
We’re seeking an experienced Solution Architect to support a large-scale Energy Assets portfolio with our client AGL, delivering end-to-end solutions across complex operational and enterprise environments.

This is a contract opportunity working across a diverse asset base including thermal, wind, hydro, and battery energy storage systems (BESS), with a strong focus on aligning technology to business strategy and future-state architecture.

This is an initial six month contract, based in Melbourne CBD in a hybrid working environment.

Key Responsibilities


Design and deliver end-to-end solution architectures across Energy Assets technology environments
Ensure solutions are fit-for-purpose, scalable, secure, and aligned to business outcomes
Provide oversight of designs, implementation quality, and adherence to architecture standards
Collaborate closely with business stakeholders, engineering teams, and vendors
Assess and validate third-party solutions, estimates, and technical approaches
Contribute to architecture forums, driving standards, patterns, and continuous improvement
Support integration across IT and Operational Technology (OT) landscapes

About You


Proven experience as a Solution Architect in complex enterprise environments
Demonstrated experience within Operational Technology (OT) environments 
Strong background in infrastructure architecture (cloud + on-prem)
Deep understanding of networking, security, identity, storage, and DR
Exposure to critical infrastructure risk frameworks (e.g. AESCSF)
Strong stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to influence at all levels
Experience working in Agile delivery environments (Scrum/Kanban)

 
Please click APPLY NOW and follow the prompts to submit your application.

Amentum is continually expanding our opportunities working on prominent defence projects across air, land and sea supporting the critical capabilities that keep Australia safe. We have a growing presence in cyber, space and nuclear programs.
  
Tell me about this role:

Working within a multidisciplinary team, you’ll play a key role in maintaining engineering governance, developing technical documentation, and supporting system verification and validation activities across the project lifecycle.
  
As a Level 2 Systems Engineer, you’ll provide clarity and structure to engineering decisions, support rigorous review of technical data, and collaborate closely with stakeholders to maintain accurate architecture and configuration documentation. You’ll help ensure system maturity, requirements traceability, and compliance with Defence standards, while contributing to risk management, system security considerations, and emerging technical challenges.


What’s involved?

Support the maintenance and currency of project engineering plans and processes, including the Engineering Management Plan (EMP) and Test and Evaluation Master Plan (TEMP).
Develop and coordinate engineering reports in accordance with project standards and direction.
Support the engineering team to conduct reviews of technical data, including CDRLs, plans, Technical Instructions, RFIs, CCPs, and ECPs.
Provide engineering input on current and emerging project risks to support project risk management activities.
Support system integration and verification and validation (V&V) activities.
Provide engineering input on design maturity and compliance against project requirements and standards.
Support the engineering team in addressing system security and Reliability, Maintainability and Availability (RMA) requirements.
  
What sort of skills do I need:

Bachelor's degree in engineering or similar qualification
Strong systems engineering capability across requirements, V&V, integration and engineering governance.
Ability to analyse and review technical data (CDRLs, RFIs, ECPs, furnished data) and support informed engineering decisions.
Proficiency in developing and maintaining engineering plans, architecture documentation, and configuration management
Skilled in stakeholder engagement, risk input, and supporting system maturity, compliance, and RMA considerations across complex projects.
Australian Citizenship,Active NV1 Clearance,ITAR Compliant
  
What’s in it for me?
Flexibility - We like our employees to have the flexibility they need to live the lives they choose. With flexible working options such as WFH where possible.
Inclusion - Our employees should feel safe bringing their whole self to work, we offer a number of programs promoting inclusion.
Benefits and Rewards – Our benefits and rewards include additional superannuation; flexible leave options; salary continuation insurance; professional certification bonus; employee discount program.
   
Amentum: Trusted Partner in National Security

Amentum is a key service provider to the Australian Government, supporting critical national security and Defence capabilities across land, air, maritime, cyber, space, nuclear, and digital domains.
  
With over 880 professionals nationwide, we deliver complex infrastructure, logistics, and technical solutions with a strong focus on quality, safety, and innovation.

With 27+ years of experience and a presence in major cities and regions, we’re proud of our people—our greatest asset. Join us and be part of a high-performing team with exceptional benefits and career opportunities. Apply directly or reach out to Symmone Chapman, sym••••••••••••@global.amentum.com

The Company:

This well known digital product leader have been growing from strength to strength and leads the way in their industry with world class customer facing  products that are AI/data driven.

With some of the strongest and most engaging products out in the market, along with internally facing products that service the business, they have been able to successfully offer robust and optimised cloud based solutions to their external and internal customer base.

Their AWS cloud based environment is one of the leaders in Australia, the ever evolving cloud platform ecosystem is constantly being enhanced particularly with AI to service and strengthen the business as a whole.

The Role:

This is an unique opportunity to join a talented cloud platform team focused on scaling cloud and AI capabilities. As a Senior AI Platform Engineer, you will play a major role in designing, building, and operating emerging AI Platforms.

Work closely with engineers, architects, and application teams on core platform initiatives.
Cloud and AI is the primary focus, define and implement secure, scalable AI platform architectures and patterns using AWS cloud services and AI/ML products.
Drive efficiency by embedding AI capabilities into core systems and workflows.
Mentor other engineers, driving modern engineering practices, cloud-native and AI approaches.
Experience:

Proven experience with full end to end ownership of design and deployment of AI-centric architectures in the cloud, including scalable, secure, and cost-optimised solutions.
Deep expertise in AWS cloud services, with strong depth in AI, ML, and data.
Experience with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools such as AWS CloudFormation, CDK.
Proven code development skills in one or more languages (Python or TypeScript), with experience integrating AI services via APIs.

Design the future of industrial automation. Deliver impact at scale.

At GEA, we don’t just engineer solutions—we power some of the world’s most critical industries. As one of the largest global suppliers to the food, beverage, and pharmaceutical sectors, our technologies drive efficiency, sustainability, and innovation across production systems worldwide.

Within our Nutrition Plant Engineering (NPE) division, we partner with customers across dairy, food, beverage, and industrial sectors to deliver end‑to‑end processing solutions—from concept and design through to commissioning and lifetime service.

We are now seeking a high‑calibre Automation Engineer to join our Melbourne team and play a key role in delivering complex, high‑value projects across Oceania.




The Opportunity

This is a hands-on project engineering role where you will take ownership of automation scope from design through to commissioning, working on cutting‑edge processing systems and digital solutions.

You will translate process requirements into robust automation architectures, develop PLC/SCADA applications, and work directly with customers and site teams to bring systems to life.

This role offers a unique blend of technical depth, project ownership, and real-world impact—ideal for engineers who enjoy seeing their work delivered on site.

Key Responsibilities
Automation Design & Development

Translate P&IDs and process descriptions into structured functional designs (IEC 848 / Grafcet)

Develop PLC programs using platforms such as Rockwell Studio and/or Siemens TIA Portal

Design and configure HMI/SCADA systems and user interfaces

Apply and maintain GEA automation standards across all project deliverables

Project Ownership & Delivery

Take ownership of automation scope across the full project lifecycle—from planning to execution

Deliver projects within agreed timelines, budgets, and quality standards

Support tendering activities through technical input, scope definition, and cost estimation

Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to ensure seamless integration and delivery

Commissioning & Site Support

Lead and support on-site commissioning, troubleshooting, and system optimisation

Provide technical support to installation and service teams, including subcontractor coordination

Deliver operator training and ensure smooth system handover to customers

Continuous Improvement & Digitalization

Identify and implement improvements to automation tools, standards, and processes

Contribute to digitalization initiatives, including remote monitoring and advanced control systems

Drive innovation to enhance system performance, reliability, and customer value

What Success Looks Like

High-quality automation solutions delivered on time and within budget

Seamless commissioning and strong customer satisfaction

Robust, scalable control systems aligned to GEA standards

Proactive contribution to innovation and digitalisation initiatives

Strong collaboration across engineering, project, and service teams

About You

You are a practical, solutions-focused Automation Engineer who enjoys working across the full project lifecycle—from design through to commissioning. You bring strong technical capability, a collaborative mindset, and the ability to deliver in complex, customer-facing environments.

You will ideally bring:

Degree in Engineering, Mechatronics, or Industrial Software (or equivalent experience)

5+ years’ experience in industrial automation or process control environments

Hands-on experience with PLC platforms (Rockwell, Siemens)

Experience with HMI/SCADA systems (e.g. Ignition, PanelView, Siemens)

Knowledge of industrial networks (Ethernet/IP, Profinet, etc.)

Strong problem-solving, communication, and stakeholder engagement skills

Willingness to travel to project sites across Australia/NZ (as required)

Why Join GEA?

Work on complex, high-impact engineering projects across critical industries

Be part of a global organization with strong local expertise

Exposure to advanced technologies and digital solutions

Opportunities for career growth, development, and international collaboration

Competitive remuneration, bonus scheme, and strong wellbeing support




Build systems that power industries. Deliver solutions that make a difference. Join GEA.

The role :

As a Data & AI Analyst /Engineer you will be involved in delivering projects based on the Microsoft Azure Data Services. This is your opportunity to help uncover, analyse and create insights and AI/ML model. Experience expert mentoring and use the opportunity to demonstrate your strengths with data. Make a real impact as, together, we empower client-lead data strategy and management. 

You will be responsible for:

Work with the Project Manager & Technical Lead to complete project tasks and to develop components as required.  
Collaborate with the Data Architect / Technical Lead and other stakeholders to understand the detailed requirements for a project. 
Ensure that all components are developed as per the implementation methodology.  
Continue to develop technical capabilities and knowledge.  
Customer Engagement & Commercial Development. 
Participate in the presales process accessing the clients’ needs if required.  
Provide inputs to technical solution design and proposal writing if required. 
To be actively involved in Brennan IT’s Best Practice and Continuous Improvement program.  
To review, create and maintain internal documentation and processes with the primary goal of achieving service delivery efficiencies. 
Skills/Experience required :

Solid Data Engineering and analysis experience  
Proven, relevant experience with Azure Data Services, MS Fabric etc
Exposure to Databricks, Snowflake or similar
Excellent verbal and written communication skills 
Good decision making / problem solving skills 
Ability to autonomously manage day-to-day client and technical issues
Ability to build and maintain relationships with customers 
Demonstrable pro-activeness 
Degree in Information Technology or similar (or equivalent experience)

Senior Data & Analytics Engineer Own the Data Backbone of an AI-Driven Tech Scaleup

Not every data engineering role gives you the chance to build the engine room.

This one does.

We’re partnering with a fast-growing Australian technology company that’s transforming a traditional industry through AI, automation, and data-driven products. With strong product-market fit, growing enterprise customers, and ambitious expansion plans, they’re now looking for a Senior Data & Analytics Engineer to take ownership of their modern data platform.

This is not a “maintain the dashboards” kind of role.

You’ll sit at the centre of a business where data powers:


real-time operational workflows,


AI-driven automation,


customer analytics,


computer vision models,


document intelligence,


and next-generation AI products.


The foundations are already in place - Snowflake, dbt, Fivetran, AWS - but the business has reached the point where they need someone senior enough to evolve the architecture, modernise pipelines, and drive the next phase of scale.

That’s where you come in.

The Opportunity You’ll join a small, highly collaborative Data & AI team in a genuinely high-ownership role.

You won’t inherit layers of process, large support teams, or narrowly defined responsibilities.

Instead, you’ll become the owner of the company’s “Data Hub” - the core platform powering everything from customer-facing reporting through to AI model training and real-time automation workflows.

One day you might be:

redesigning real-time data pipelines,


optimising Snowflake and ETL architecture,


improving governance and access controls,


enabling natural-language analytics experiences,


or building pipelines for OCR, PDF extraction, and computer vision workloads.

Why This Role Is Different The business is specifically looking for someone who can think strategically while remaining deeply hands-on.

You’ll have the autonomy to:

shape architectural direction,


influence platform strategy,


solve real business problems,


and build solutions yourself.


This is a role for someone who enjoys ownership and wants their work to directly influence product capability, AI innovation, and business growth.

The Tech Stack Core Technologies
Snowflake


dbt


Fivetran


AWS (S3, Athena, EFS, QuickSight)


SQL


Python


Supabase


Key Focus Areas
ETL / ELT pipelines


Real-time data workflows


Data modelling & governance


AI-OCR & Document AI


Computer Vision pipelines


Customer analytics & BI


AI/ML data enablement


What You’ll Be Working On ETL Modernisation
Help redesign current batch-based data architecture into a more real-time capable environment that supports AI automation and operational workflows. Send your resume to ree••@theonset.com.au

Modern Reporting & Analytics Drive the next generation of customer-facing analytics and self-service reporting, including natural language querying experiences.

Data Governance & Security Strengthen access controls, governance frameworks, and platform compliance while improving scalability and operational maturity.

AI & Unstructured Data Build and optimise pipelines for documents, PDFs, images, OCR, and computer vision workloads that directly power AI products.

What They’re Looking For You’re likely someone who:

has strong end-to-end data engineering experience,


enjoys autonomy and ownership,


thrives in startup or scaleup environments,


and is excited by AI-enabled products and unstructured data problems.


You don’t need to be an ML Engineer - but you do need to understand how modern AI systems consume and rely on data.

Ideal Experience
5+ years in Data Engineering / Analytics Engineering


Strong Snowflake expertise


Advanced SQL & Python


Experience designing scalable data architectures


Exposure to real-time or event-driven pipelines


Experience with OCR, document processing, or computer vision pipelines is highly valued


Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills


If you’re excited by the idea of building a modern data platform at the intersection of AI, automation, analytics, and product innovation - we’d love to hear from you. Send your resume to ree••@theonset.com.au

Salary: Be part of a significant Defence program
Ad Text:
About us

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation. As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world's most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.
 

About the program
AIR6500 is the RAAF’s $3 billion program to develop a joint air battle management system that builds on the capabilities offered by Next Generation sensor and command and control systems. AIR6500 will create a fully networked ‘system of systems’ that synchronises air and missile defence operations in joint and coalition environments, maximising situational awareness and enhancing the speed of decision making across the battlespace.


About the role

Take your career to new heights with Lockheed Martin Australia as a System Safety Engineer on the AIR6500 T2B FADS program, a next-generation defence capability that will transform Australia’s air and missile defence. In this key role, you'll help shape system safety outcomes across complex and critical technologies.

What you’ll do
Working under the guidance of the System Safety Lead, you will:

Assess system designs and procedures to ensure safety requirements are well defined and maintained

Conduct hazard analyses and safety risk assessments independently

Develop technical documentation to support system certification and test readiness

Collaborate closely with subject matter experts across engineering disciplines, including ILS, HSI, software, and cyber

Contribute to technical investigations, planning, scheduling, and estimation of safety engineering tasks

This role offers location flexibility and can be performed from either Adelaide, Canberra, Melbourne or Newcastle.

 

What you bring
We’re looking for someone with a sharp analytical mindset and a collaborative approach. Ideally, you will have: 

Strong experience in System Safety Engineering in Defence or a similar complex environment

Familiarity with safety standards like MIL-STD-882E and a solid understanding of the Systems Engineering lifecycle

Proven ability to analyse technical risks and produce clear, high-quality safety documentation

Knowledge of safety and risk management frameworks

Strong communication skills and the ability to work cross-functionally

Eligibility for or possession of an Australian NV-1 security clearance and ability to work with Export Controlled material 

Bonus points for:

Experience with Model-Based Systems Development (MBSD)

A track record of working effectively with diverse stakeholder groups 

Join a high-performing team where your work will have national impact, and your development will be supported every step of the way.


How We Reward and Support You

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.

We offer you a range of benefits built to suit you, no matter what stage of life you are in. 

A variety of flexible work options

Access to our online discounts program offering immediate discounts or cashback

A variety of leave options including Purchase Leave to support you and your family  

Additional benefits such as Annual Flu Vaccinations, Novated Leasing and Fitness Programs


Special Requirements

Applicants must be eligible to obtain and maintain an Australian Defence Security Clearance and employment may be contingent on the provision of further personal information including citizenship to ensure compliance with international export control legislation and regulations.

A Veteran-friendly Workplace

Veterans are encouraged to apply. We highly value the unique skills and experiences that veterans and transitioning service members can contribute to our team. We are proud to be recognised as a Veteran Friendly Employer by the Australian Government.

Equal Opportunity Statement

Lockheed Martin Australia supports Equal Employment Opportunity and values diversity in the workplace by creating a place of employment that is, free of unlawful discrimination and is harassment free, fair, ethical, inclusive, and values the contribution of all its employees. By leveraging our employees' unique talents and experiences, we will deliver innovative, affordable solutions and unparalleled customer value.

As part of our endeavours to increase participation of Indigenous Australians, we welcome applications from all people, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.

We are committed to creating a workplace where everyone can thrive. If you are selected for this role, we would be happy to discuss any accommodations that will help you to be successful.
